data.requirement: Remove normalization of CLTV/revenue against population density
data.reason: Analysis shows all customers are within California with limited zip code spread. Geographical comparisons are better handled through basic spatial aggregation rather than population normalization which adds minimal insight

visualization.requirement: Replace scatter plot matrix with pairwise scatter plots of cltv vs total_revenue vs tenure with color encoding by customer_status
visualization.reason: Scatter plot matrices become unreadable with 7k+ points. Pairwise comparisons maintain correlation analysis while allowing better point density control through opacity/binning

visualization.requirement: Simplify to basic geospatial heatmap showing churn_value density using latitude/longitude coordinates
visualization.reason: Population density contours require advanced GIS processing unavailable in Vega-Lite. Basic heatmap maintains geographic pattern detection without technical errors

interaction.requirement: Update to use customer_status filter across all views instead of churn_score slider
interaction.reason: Ensures consistent encoding between scatter plots (color by status) and filter control (status selection). Avoids mixed use of categorical/continuous filters

interaction.requirement: Change to brush-based selection on zip_code clusters instead of city-level drill down
interaction.reason: Original city-level aggregation insufficiently granular. Direct spatial selection better leverages available coordinates while avoiding hierarchical data issues
